original_image_tags: A faded, aged document page with visible crease lines and punch holes at the top. The primary text is largely illegible due to fading or possibly being the reverse side of another page. At the top, reversed text is visible, appearing to read "L. A. HENNESSEY" and "FLYING SAUCER INVESTIGATIONS" when viewed from the correct orientation. Faint, illegible typed text covers most of the page. In the bottom left corner, there are handwritten annotations and a faint red stamp.

elements.description: The page is mostly the blank back of a document with faint reverse text showing through. Visible on the back are: a faint rectangular outline in the upper left corner, three hole-punch marks at the top, several handwritten and stamped annotations at the bottom including 'JAN 22 12:05 PM '65', 'FBI', and 'REC'D-PRESS & BURNS'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual elements (faded back of a page, hole punches, handwritten/stamped annotations at bottom), but Description B adds specific quoted stamp text ('JAN 22 12:05 PM '65', 'FBI', 'REC'D-PRESS & BURNS') that Description A does not mention, creating an addition without direct contradiction.
